caption: Plot 14 has tomatoes coming in faster than I expected. Left a bag at Beth's door this morning. Seven beefsteaks.
The last of the winter greens out of the cold frame. Better than anything the store has this time of year.
They're saying 91 by Tuesday. Watered the beds early before the heat settles in.
Morning walk at Roger Williams Park. Frank set a brisk pace and I made him slow down by the lake.
Later start than usual. It was already humid by seven-thirty and I didn't push it.
Friday lunch at Chelo's. Same booth, same clam chowder, as it has been for years.
Back to the garden before the afternoon sun. The peas are finally climbing.
A quiet Tuesday. Library in the morning, then the garden. I'll take more of those.
Finished 'Iron and Olive' last night. Stayed up far too late for the last forty pages. Worth it.
Sorted three carts of donations at the library this morning. The history shelf is finally in order again.
First hard frost tonight, so I pulled the last of the peppers. Roasting a tray of them for the freezer.
Lily's class did a play about the colonists today. She had two whole lines and delivered them like a pro.
Coltrane on the record player and a pot of soup on the stove. A good kind of grey Sunday.
Tom called from Denver — eighteen inches of snow out there. Hard to picture from rainy Cranston.
Library book sale planning meeting ran long, but we have the spring date locked in. Mark your calendars.
Jack lost his first tooth at our table tonight. The tooth fairy rate has gone up considerably since my day.
Garlic and shallots are in the ground for spring. Mulched the bed before the cold snap settles in.
Started Monk's 'Brilliant Corners' again this morning. Forty years of listening and it still surprises me.
Friday lunch at Chelo's with Frank. Same booth, same clam chowder, decades running.
Beth and the kids came over for Sunday dinner. The house is quiet again and the dishwasher is humming.
Reorganized the bookshelf into history, philosophy, and fiction at last. Four books queued for the month.
